Resolving this contradiction is necessary for the artifact to be logically coherent, making this dimension load-bearing.","R1.4":"The abstract positions LIRA against two weak baselines (always-route-to-vector, a static rule-of-thumb) but cites no prior learned routing or index-tuning work. Strengthening novelty positioning is a central expected task.","R1.6":"'Make stronger' in a writing context standardly includes sentence-level impact, precision, and rhetorical force — e.g., the advisor-mode paragraph currently buries the 22% memory-pressure result as a subordinate clause."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Does 'stronger' primarily mean improving prose quality and rhetorical impact (sentence clarity, emphasis, narrative punch), or diagnosing and repairing logical and argumentative weaknesses (e.g., the stateless-vs-sliding-window contradiction, unsupported novelty claims, missing prior-work anchors)?"}

rationale.R1.1: The contribution claim and gap statement are present but underdeveloped; 'make stronger' plausibly requires sharpening the thesis and the problem framing (why hybrid LLM-backed systems are underserved by existing routers).
rationale.R1.2: There is a load-bearing internal contradiction: the abstract explicitly claims the router is 'stateless across queries' with 'no workload memory or aggregate statistics carried across requests,' yet a later paragraph describes maintaining a sliding-window join-selectivity histogram refreshed every 500 queries — a clear stateful mechanism. Resolving this contradiction is necessary for the artifact to be logically coherent, making this dimension load-bearing.
rationale.R1.4: The abstract positions LIRA against two weak baselines (always-route-to-vector, a static rule-of-thumb) but cites no prior learned routing or index-tuning work. Strengthening novelty positioning is a central expected task.
rationale.R1.6: 'Make stronger' in a writing context standardly includes sentence-level impact, precision, and rhetorical force — e.g., the advisor-mode paragraph currently buries the 22% memory-pressure result as a subordinate clause.

parsed.clarification_question: Does 'stronger' primarily mean improving prose quality and rhetorical impact (sentence clarity, emphasis, narrative punch), or diagnosing and repairing logical and argumentative weaknesses (e.g., the stateless-vs-sliding-window contradiction, unsupported novelty claims, missing prior-work anchors)?
